creator: bscott@bunyatech.com.au
text:
Running GENERIC kernel downloaded as part of the latest snapshot build at http://ftp.freebsd.org/pub/FreeBSD/snapshots/arm64/aarch64/ISO-IMAGES/12.0/FreeBSD-12.0-CURRENT-arm64-aarch64-RPI3-20180618-r335317.img.xz.

My kgdb skills are non-existent however I have saved the crash to a dump file and installed the gdb package to get kgdb. I have also checked out the source tree for the correct revision for reference from the debugger. Current status is:

# kgdb -n 0 /boot/kernel/kernel 
GNU gdb (GDB) 8.1 [GDB v8.1 for FreeBSD]

There is NO WARRANTY, to the extent permitted by law.  Type "show copying"
and "show warranty" for details.
This GDB was configured as "aarch64-portbld-freebsd12.0".
Type "show configuration" for configuration details.
For bug reporting instructions, please see:
<http://www.gnu.org/software/gdb/bugs/>.
Find the GDB manual and other documentation resources online at:
<http://www.gnu.org/software/gdb/documentation/>.
For help, type "help".
Type "apropos word" to search for commands related to "word"...
Reading symbols from /boot/kernel/kernel...Reading symbols from /usr/lib/debug//boot/kernel/kernel.debug...done.

warning: Breakpoint address adjusted from 0xffff0000006923c4 to 0xff0000006923c4.

warning: Breakpoint address adjusted from 0xffff0000006923c4 to 0xff0000006923c4.
done.

warning: Breakpoint address adjusted from 0xffff0000006923c4 to 0xff0000006923c4.

warning: Breakpoint address adjusted from 0xffff0000006923c4 to 0xff0000006923c4.
inferior.c:309: internal-error: struct inferior *find_inferior_pid(int): Assertion `pid != 0' failed.
A problem internal to GDB has been detected,
further debugging may prove unreliable.
Quit this debugging session? (y or n) n

This is a bug, please report it.  For instructions, see:
<http://www.gnu.org/software/gdb/bugs/>.

inferior.c:309: internal-error: struct inferior *find_inferior_pid(int): Assertion `pid != 0' failed.
A problem internal to GDB has been detected,
further debugging may prove unreliable.
Create a core file of GDB? (y or n) n
Command aborted.
(kgdb) l *dyn_tick+0x7c
No symbol "dyn_tick" in current context.
(kgdb)

I have also tried following one set of instructions to set up a .gdbinit file by rebuilding the kernel and doing 'make gdbinit' in the relevant directory. Using that file causes problems with variable remotebaud not found (easily commented out since I am local), and command asf not found (recently removed from the system). The result is the same (i.e. No symbol "dyn_tick" in current context.).

I don't know if this is an artefact of the internal error in gdb, the change to the debugging environment, or just my lack of knowledge of the debugging process for the kernel.

I'm very happy to try anything out, particularly as I now have the vmcore file and don't need to crash the machine (I need to be physically present to connect a serial cable to my laptop to interact with ddb).
